human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for cannabis-specific execution fidelity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, CCTT and manifest traceability, packaging and recall stop conditions, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to cannabis order, package, manifest, delivery-ledger, recall, packaging, and closure-evidence handling rather than generic store operations.
Authority sources cover DCC retail, delivery, packaging, recall, consumer notice, and consumer-law guidance plus CPSC child-resistant packaging controls and Metrc transfer workflow references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, unsupported completion, package and manifest mismatch, recall stop conditions, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence handling, conflicting policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/cannabis-and-controlled-substance-operations/cannabis-operations-specialist/ so the lane remains distinct from dispensary operations, patient services, controlled inventory, compliance, quality, and analytics.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around medical advice, legal interpretation, recalled-product release, inventory adjustments, and transfer or quality authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated cannabis operations specialist for package-controlled fulfillment, delivery-ledger handling, recall-aware execution, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
